parsed_review.summary: CP00-877 (RP29.P02.M04.S03–S12, risk A, 10 units, Secondary Workflow Slice) closes the RP29 Commercial Readiness secondary-workflow boundary as a descriptor-only slice. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level production_ready advancement is substantively supported: (1) Implementation is descriptor-only — packages/commercial/src/{service,policies,registry,model,states,validators}.js contain no fs/writeFile/child_process/spawn/fetch/http/process.env/eval; all factory outputs are Object.freeze'd and every boundary flag (runtime_execution, writes_product_state, persists_product_state, permission_decision_written, audit_event_written, runtime_lock_acquired) is false; audit hint sets writes_audit_event_now:false; lock rule is declared_not_acquired; persistence_boundary is no_persistence; idempotency_scope is tenant_matter_action. (2) No real client data, credentials, or secrets — only synthetic tenant/Matter identifiers; the 6 no-leak guards are present in contract. (3) Coverage matches the live plan exactly — closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-877 included_units, binding (registry.js COMMERCIAL_CP877_PACK_BINDING = SERVICE_ROWS.slice(2,12)), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and baseline all agree on 10 RP29 units with deliverable distribution implementation:6/security_audit:2/ui:2 and micro_title Secondary Workflow Slice:10. (4) Tests are CP877-specific and comprehensive (service.test.js + model.test.js cover tenant/Matter prechecks, no-write audit hint, cross-tenant denial, review/approval routing, idempotency, lock, persistence); the targeted `node --test packages/commercial/test/*.test.js` and `npm run rp29:commercial:validate` both recorded exit 0 in command-evidence.json. (5) The on-disk contract is internally consistent (validation.valid=true; required_capabilities, safety_gates, no_leak_guards, mandatory_artifacts match registry.js) and is re-validated by model.test.js via validateCommercialReadinessContract with errors []. (6) Authority boundary is correct everywhere — claude_is_final_approval:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, claude_packet.read_only:true; the Hermes packet is deterministic evidence only (emits_runtime_receipt:false) with no Hermes runtime embedded in product source. (7) Review evidence is valid per CP178+ hardening — auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1456-byte well-formed JSON); no prior invalid review attempts; the current 0-byte/pending claude-review-result.json is the expected pre-review placeholder, not a failed attempt. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer, not the final approver. The pack honestly reports production_ready:false/runtime_ready:false/status:in_progress while pending this review plus the downstream deterministic validation ladder, adjudication, and inspection finalization — those are separate automated gates that run after this review by design, so they are not treated as blocking findings here, and the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim (so blocks_goal_closeout=false). One non-blocking P3 observation: the contract's generated_by label names a validate-only script. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; this review does not block pack closeout.

findings.title: Contract generated_by names a validate-only script (cosmetic provenance label)
findings.details: The contract's generated_by (line 3) and createCommercialReadinessContractProjection() in packages/commercial/src/registry.js (line ~1158) both name scripts/validate-rp29-commercial-readiness-contract.mjs as the generator, but that script only reads and validates the contract (readFile + validate* helpers, process.exit on error) and never writes the file. A grep of scripts/ finds no writer of commercial-readiness-contract.json. The contract content is nonetheless verified correct by packages/commercial/test/model.test.js (validateCommercialReadinessContract returns errors []), so this is a cosmetic provenance-label inaccuracy, not a content, boundary, no-write, or no-real-data defect. It is pre-existing and consistent across the CP873–CP877 RP29 series.
findings.recommendation: Either point generated_by at the actual contract writer/process, or rename the field (e.g., validated_by) to reflect that the named script validates rather than emits the artifact. Non-blocking for CP00-877; suitable for cleanup in a future RP29 contract-generation pack.
findings.post_review_disposition: Recorded as non-blocking P3. Does not affect the descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data boundary or the production_ready advancement; no change required for CP00-877 closeout.
